rulings.Case Title: COMMONWEALTH LAND TITLE INSURANCE COMPANY VS. FEDEX OFFICE AND PRINT SERVICES, INC et al (PROVIDE ACCESS)
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Leave To File X-Complaint/Order Shortening Time
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, September 23, 2013, line 1. -DEFENDANTS FEDEX OFFICE AND PRINT SERVICES'MOTION For Leave To File Cross Complaint/Order Shortening Time. Defendant's Motion for Leave to File Cross-Complaint is GRANTED as follows: (1) FedEx Office and Print Services, Inc may not seek a continuance of the trial based on any issues arising out of cross-complaint; and (2) should the Cross-Defendant make a motion to continue trial, FedEx Office and Print Services, Inc must within 5 court days of such request seek a separate trial on its Cross-Complaint. Defendant and its counsel are ordered to comply with SFLR 2.6B in future. = (501/HMD)

rulings.Case Title: BANK OF AMERICA, NATIONAL ASSOCIATION AS SUCCESSOR VS. STEPHANIE NAIFEH et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, September 23, 2013, line 2. -PLAINTIFF BANK OF AMERICA'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Plaintiff's Motion for Summary Judgment is DENIED, Plaintiff failed to shift burden re: Material Facts 2, 4, 5, 9-23. = (501/HMD)

rulings.Case Title: BANK OF AMERICA, NATIONAL ASSOCIATION AS SUCCESSOR VS. STEPHANIE NAIFEH et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notcie Of Motion And Motion For Issuance Of Protective Order Against The Requests For Admissions, Special Interrogatiries, Form Interrogatories, And Requests For Production Served Upon Pltf
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, September 23, 2013, line 2. -PLAINTIFF BANK OF AMERICA'S Motion For Issuance Of Protective Order Against The Requests For Admissions, Special Interrogatiries, Form Interrogatories, And Requests For Production Served Upon Plaintiff. Plaintiff's Motion for Issuance of Protective Order against the Requests for Admissions, Special Interrogatories, and Requests for Production. Hearing Required. Parties are ordered to appear at 9:00 a.m. to engage in meaningful meet and confer efforts. = (501/HMD)

rulings.Case Title: PAUL WILEY VS. ADAM BRISTOL et al

rulings.Case Title: PAUL WILEY VS. ADAM BRISTOL et al

rulings.Case Title: DAVID ARAKELIAN VS. WELLS FARGO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Dismiss Action For Failure To Amend Within The Time Allowed By The Court
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, September 23, 2013, line 4. -DEFENDANT WELLS FARGO BANK'S Motion To Dismiss Action For Failure To Amend Within The Time Allowed By The Court. Defendant's Motion to Dismiss for Failure to Amend is continued to October 11, 2013. Defendant is orderd to provide a courtesy copy of the document referenced in 3 of Kamka Declaration in support of the motion - "A Notice of Entry of Order was signed by the Court on May 6, 2013. (501/HMD)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID ARAKELIAN VS. WELLS FARGO et al

rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, September 23, 2013, line 4. -DEFENDANT WELLS FARGO BANK'S Motion To Expunge Lis Pendens. rulings.Case Title: DIANA D. SAM VS. CATALINA BOYKIN et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, September 23, 2013, line 5.- DEFENDANT ARTURO QUIZMUNDO DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Defendant's Demurrer to Complaint is continued to October 17, 2013. Moving party is ordered to comply with SFLR 2.6C and provide courtesy copy of the operative complaint to department 501 no later than October 3, 2013 with a cover letter reflecting new hearing date. = (501/HMD)

rulings.Case Title: EPMI, A BAYSIDE COMPANY, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. BRENDA BROOKS et al

rulings.Case Title: SALLY LIU VS. ESPETACION MEDINA et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, September 23, 2013, line 8. -DEFENDANTS ESPETACION MEDINA AND JOSE CERONS' DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendants' Demurrer to First Amended Complaint is SUSTAINED without leave to amend. Notice is fatally defective and demands amounts in excess of the rent due. = (501/HMD)

rulings.Case Title: ERLINDA ONG LOREDO VS. BENJAMIN ONG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Ervice Of Summons And Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, September 23, 2013, line 9. -DEFENDANTS BENJAMIN ONG, LOURDES ONG, ANDREW ONG AND JONATHAN ONGS' Motion To Quash Ervice Of Summons And Complaint. Defendant Benjamin Ong's Motion to Quash Service of Summons and Complaint is GRANTED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to attach a proof of service indicating the date and time of mailing, or unequivocally declare that both posting and mailing were accomplished on August 22, 2013 at 7:54 p.m. Defendant's counsel is ordered again (See CUD-13-644782 minute order 6/26/13) to comply with SFLR 2.6C in future. = (501/HMD)

rulings.Case Title: COBASAC LLC VS. REBECCA HILL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Quash Service Of Unlawful Detainer Summons
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, September 23, 2013, line 10. DEFENDANT REBECCA HILL'S Notice Of Motion To Quash Service Of Unlawful Detainer Summons. Defendant's Motion to Quash Service of Summons is GRANTED, with leave to amend to attach a complete copy of the lease in compliance with CCP 1166. = (501/HMD)
